We defer discussion of graph neural network models until section 13.4 and first describe the types of problems these networks tackle and their associated loss functions. Supervised graph problems usually fall into one of three categories (figure 13.6).

Graph-level tasks: The network assigns a label or estimates one or more values from the entire graph, exploiting both the structure and node embeddings. For example, we might want to predict the temperature at which a molecule becomes liquid (a regression task

For graph-level tasks, the output node embeddings are combined (e.g., by averaging), and the resulting vector is mapped via a linear transformation or neural network to a fixed-size vector. For regression, the mismatch between the result and the ground truth v
